title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: R code quality addressed many tools. honest overview existing landscape rsonar fits .

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: lintr (CRAN) reference R linter, maintained r-lib community. detects: Style errors (naming, indentation, line length) Anti-patterns (T instead TRUE, 1:length(x) instead seq_along(x)) Excessive cyclomatic complexity Dead commented-code Limitations standalone usage: lintr produces results console text files, without aggregation, visual reporting, debt calculation.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: styler (CRAN) applies tidyverse style guide. can reformat code automatically (style_dir()) check compliance without modification (dry = ""). Limitations standalone usage: consolidated reporting, native CI integration.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: covr (CRAN) measures test coverage line line. exports Codecov, Coveralls Cobertura (GitLab CI format). Limitations standalone usage: raw numeric results, configurable Quality Gate threshold.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: goodpractice (GitHub) checks ~25 R best practices: unused dependencies, deprecated functions, T_and_F_symbol, etc. Limitations standalone usage: package-oriented , work plain scripts.

previous_headings: Alternatives to rsonar in the R ecosystem
title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: r-lib/actions offers pre-built GitHub actions (check-r-package, test-coverage, lint). Excellent GitHub integration, : GitLab natively supported consolidated HTML report debt calculation configurable Quality Gate

previous_headings: Alternatives to rsonar in the R ecosystem
title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: rcmdcheck runs R CMD check captures results. comprehensive CRAN package validation, : handle style technical debt Raw, non-visual results

previous_headings: Alternatives to rsonar in the R ecosystem
title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: SonarQube supports R since version 9.x via community plugin sonar-r-plugin. plugin : Poorly maintained (last significant activity 2021) Limited rules (< 20 rules vs 100+ lintr) Requires dedicated SonarQube instance

previous_headings: Alternatives to rsonar in the R ecosystem
title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: codemetar generates package metadata performs quality analysis.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: One function, sonar_analyse(), replaces 4 separate calls. result coherent S3 object can passed sonar_report(), quality_gate(), export_junit(), export_sarif(), etc.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: sonar_report() generates HTML report without server, without Docker, without database. opens browser can archived CI artifact.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: SQALE model (Software Quality Assessment based Lifecycle Expectations) used SonarQube: Rating=f(Debt (min)Base effort (min))\text{Rating} = f\left(\frac{\text{Debt (min)}}{\text{Base effort (min)}}\right) category (lint errors, style, missing coverage…) contributes total debt minutes, allowing prioritize fixes.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
code: # Block CI if coverage drops below 80% or lint errors exist quality_gate(res,   coverage_min    = 80,   lint_errors_max = 0,   fail_on_error   = TRUE  # quit(status = 1) in CI )

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: export_sonar_json() produces file compatible SonarQube’s Generic Issue Import. Organizations existing SonarQube instance Java/Python can inject R results without additional plugin.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: export_sarif() outputs industry-standard SARIF format, integrates directly GitHub Code Scanning, VS Code, Azure DevOps.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: sonar_diff() compares two analyses detect regressions, sonar_trend() persists metrics JSON file historical tracking — just like SonarQube’s project history.

what: 8. Works on scripts, not just packages
title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: Unlike goodpractice rcmdcheck, rsonar analyzes directory containing .R files, even without DESCRIPTION.

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: quality_score() gives developers immediate percentage rating directly IDE, without server CI workflow:
code: quality_score(".")

title: Alternatives and Added Value of rsonar
text: lintr — tool , comprehensive megalinter — orchestrates linters 50+ languages Docker sonar-scanner — reference Quality Gate model qodana — JetBrains code inspector, inspiration HTML report r-lib/actions — reference R CI integration

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
text: rsonar integrates CI pipelines following model SonarQube: Quality Gate fails, pipeline blocked (exit code 1) merge rejected.
code: Code push → Lint → Style → Tests + Coverage → Quality Gate → Report

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
text: simplest configuration: single job analyzes everything fails gate pass. copy template project:
code: # .gitlab-ci.yml include:   # Use the template provided by rsonar   - project: 'ddotta/rsonar'     ref: main     file: '/inst/templates/ci/gitlab-rsonar.yml' library(rsonar) use_rsonar_ci("gitlab")

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
code: # .gitlab-ci.yml image: rocker/r-base:4.5.1  stages:   - lint   - style   - coverage   - quality_gate   - report  variables:   # Customize thresholds here   COVERAGE_MIN: "80"   LINT_ERRORS_MAX: "0"  before_script:   - |     R -q -e "     pkgs <- c('remotes','lintr','styler','covr','goodpractice',               'cli','jsonlite','xml2','glue','fs','rlang','withr','testthat')     miss <- pkgs[!(pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages()))]     if (length(miss) > 0) install.packages(miss, repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')     remotes::install_local('.', dependencies=FALSE, quiet=TRUE)     "  rsonar-lint:   stage: lint   script:     - |       Rscript -e "       library(rsonar)       res <- sonar_analyse('.', include_coverage=FALSE, include_goodpractice=FALSE)       export_junit(res, 'junit-lint.xml')       export_sonar_json(res, 'sonar-issues.json')       if (res\$metrics\$n_lint_errors > 0) stop('Lint errors detected')       "   artifacts:     when: always     reports:       junit: junit-lint.xml     paths:       - sonar-issues.json  rsonar-style:   stage: style   script:     - |       Rscript -e "       library(rsonar)       res <- sonar_analyse('.', include_lint=FALSE,                                include_coverage=FALSE,                                include_goodpractice=FALSE)       if (res\$metrics\$n_style_issues > 0)         stop(res\$metrics\$n_style_issues, ' file(s) not formatted')       "  rsonar-coverage:   stage: coverage   script:     - |       Rscript -e "       library(rsonar)       res <- sonar_analyse('.', include_lint=FALSE, include_style=FALSE,                                include_goodpractice=FALSE)       pct <- res\$metrics\$coverage_pct       cat('Coverage:', pct, '%\n')       if (!is.na(pct)) covr::to_cobertura(res\$coverage, 'coverage.xml')       "   coverage: '/Coverage: ([0-9.]+) %/'   artifacts:     reports:       coverage_report:         coverage_format: cobertura         path: coverage.xml  rsonar-gate:   stage: quality_gate   script:     - |       Rscript -e "       library(rsonar)       res <- sonar_analyse('.')       quality_gate(res,         coverage_min     = as.numeric(Sys.getenv('COVERAGE_MIN', '80')),         lint_errors_max  = as.integer(Sys.getenv('LINT_ERRORS_MAX', '0')),         style_issues_max = 0,         rating_min       = 'C',         fail_on_error    = TRUE)       "  rsonar-report:   stage: report   when: always   script:     - |       Rscript -e "       library(rsonar)       res <- sonar_analyse('.')       sonar_report(res, output='rsonar-report.html', open=FALSE)       sonar_trend(res)       "   artifacts:     paths:       - rsonar-report.html       - rsonar-history.json     expire_in: 4 weeks

what: Integration with an existing SonarQube instance
title: Complete CI/CD Integration
text: SonarQube instance already handles Java/Python, can send R results :
code: sonar-scan:   stage: quality_gate   image: sonarsource/sonar-scanner-cli:latest   script:     - sonar-scanner       -Dsonar.projectKey=my-r-project       -Dsonar.sources=R/       -Dsonar.externalIssuesReportPaths=sonar-issues.json       -Dsonar.host.url=$SONAR_HOST_URL       -Dsonar.login=$SONAR_TOKEN   needs: [rsonar-lint]

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
code: # Copy the workflow to .github/workflows/rsonar.yml use_rsonar_ci("github")

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
code: # .github/workflows/rsonar.yml name: rsonar — R Code Quality  on:   push:     branches: [main, master]   pull_request:     branches: [main, master]  jobs:   rsonar:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest     steps:       - uses: actions/checkout@v5        - uses: r-lib/actions/setup-r@v2         with:           r-version: "release"        - name: Install rsonar and dependencies         run: |           Rscript -e "           remotes::install_github('ddotta/rsonar')           "        - name: Full analysis         run: |           Rscript -e "           library(rsonar)           res <- sonar_analyse('.')                      # HTML report (always)           sonar_report(res, output='rsonar-report.html', open=FALSE)           export_junit(res, 'junit-results.xml')           export_sarif(res, 'rsonar.sarif')                      # Quality Gate (blocks on failure)           quality_gate(res,             coverage_min    = 80,             lint_errors_max = 0,             fail_on_error   = TRUE)           "        - name: Upload SARIF to GitHub Code Scanning         if: always()         uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3         with:           sarif_file: rsonar.sarif        - name: Publish JUnit results         if: always()         uses: mikepenz/action-junit-report@v5         with:           report_paths: junit-results.xml        - name: Upload HTML report         if: always()         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4         with:           name: rsonar-report           path: rsonar-report.html

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
code: // Jenkinsfile pipeline {     agent {         docker {             image 'rocker/r-base:4.5.1'         }     }     stages {         stage('Install rsonar') {             steps {                 sh '''                 Rscript -e "remotes::install_github('ddotta/rsonar')"                 '''             }         }         stage('Quality analysis') {             steps {                 sh '''                 Rscript -e "                 library(rsonar)                 res <- sonar_analyse('.')                 sonar_report(res, output='rsonar-report.html', open=FALSE)                 export_junit(res, 'junit-results.xml')                 quality_gate(res, coverage_min=80, lint_errors_max=0, fail_on_error=TRUE)                 "                 '''             }             post {                 always {                     junit 'junit-results.xml'                     publishHTML([                         reportDir:   '.',                         reportFiles: 'rsonar-report.html',                         reportName:  'rsonar Quality Report'                     ])                 }             }         }     } }

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
text: make easy run locally pushing: want quick local indicator IDE, run:
code: library(rsonar) quality_score(".") # Makefile .PHONY: quality lint style coverage report  quality:     Rscript -e "library(rsonar); res <- sonar_analyse('.'); print(res)"  lint:     Rscript -e "library(rsonar); res <- sonar_analyse('.', include_style=FALSE, include_coverage=FALSE, include_goodpractice=FALSE); print(res$$lint)"  style:     Rscript -e "styler::style_dir('R')"  coverage:     Rscript -e "library(rsonar); res <- sonar_analyse('.', include_lint=FALSE, include_style=FALSE); cat('Coverage:', res$$metrics$$coverage_pct, '%\n')"  report:     Rscript -e "library(rsonar); res <- sonar_analyse('.'); sonar_report(res)"

title: Complete CI/CD Integration
text: project already many issues, don’t block everything immediately:
code: # Phase 1: observe without blocking quality_gate(res, lint_errors_max = Inf, style_issues_max = Inf,              coverage_min = 0, fail_on_error = FALSE)  # Phase 2: block on new critical errors only quality_gate(res, lint_errors_max = 5, style_issues_max = Inf,              coverage_min = 0)  # Phase 3: strict gate quality_gate(res, lint_errors_max = 0, style_issues_max = 0,              coverage_min = 80, fail_on_error = TRUE)

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: Teams developing Python, Java JavaScript benefit SonarQube: centralized tool analyzes code quality, measures test coverage, detects code smells calculates technical debt. R developers, however, must juggle several disparate tools. rsonar bridges gap orchestrating best R tools available:

title: Introduction to rsonar
code: # From GitHub (recommended) remotes::install_github("ddotta/rsonar")

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: sonar_analyse() function: Lists .R files, excluding cache directories (renv, packrat, etc.) Runs lintr::lint_dir() using project’s .lintr file present Checks style styler::style_file(..., dry = "") Measures coverage covr::package_coverage() Runs goodpractice::gp() package Computes technical debt using SQALE model
code: library(rsonar)  # Full analysis (from the root of an R package) res <- sonar_analyse("path/to/my/package")

title: Introduction to rsonar
code: print(res)

what: 2bis. Get a quick quality percentage in your IDE
title: Introduction to rsonar
text: rapid local feedback development (without CI): default, quality_score() runs fast analysis (coverage goodpractice disabled) displays percentage score plus SQALE rating.
code: quality_score(".") ── rsonar — Quality Report ────────────────────────────────── ℹ Project  : /home/user/mypackage ℹ Analysis : 2026-04-20 14:32 ℹ Files    : 8 R file(s)  ── Metrics ──────────────────────────────────────────────────   Lint        : 3 issue(s) (1 err / 2 warn / 0 style)   Style       : 1 non-compliant file(s)   Coverage    : 72.4%   Goodpractice: 2 failure(s)  ── Technical Debt ─────────────────────────────────────────── 🟡 SQALE rating: C ⏱  Estimated duration: 1.42h (85 min)

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: HTML report contains: Dashboard consolidated metrics SQALE →E rating Lint issues list severity, file, line rule Improperly formatted files detected styler Technical debt breakdown category minutes
code: sonar_report(res, output = "quality.html")

title: Introduction to rsonar
code: gate <- quality_gate(res,   coverage_min     = 80,   # 80% minimum coverage   lint_errors_max  = 0,    # 0 errors tolerated   style_issues_max = 0,    # all code must be formatted   rating_min       = "C"   # minimum SQALE rating ) print(gate) ── Quality Gate: PASSED ────────────────────────────────────── ✔ Coverage >= 80% [82.1%] ✔ Lint errors <= 0 [0] ✔ Style issues <= 0 [0] ✔ SQALE rating >= C [B]

title: Introduction to rsonar
code: # Add the recommended rsonar .lintr use_rsonar_lintr()  # Add a pre-configured GitLab CI pipeline use_rsonar_ci("gitlab")  # Or GitHub Actions use_rsonar_ci("github")

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: rsonar also works projects R packages:
code: res <- sonar_analyse(   "path/to/project",   include_coverage     = FALSE,  # no tests/   include_goodpractice = FALSE   # no DESCRIPTION ) sonar_report(res)

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: Use sonar_diff() compare two analyses detect regressions:
code: baseline <- sonar_analyse(".", include_coverage = FALSE) # ... make changes ... current  <- sonar_analyse(".", include_coverage = FALSE) diff     <- sonar_diff(current, baseline) print(diff)

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: Use sonar_trend() CI pipeline build history quality metrics:
code: res <- sonar_analyse(".") sonar_trend(res, file = "rsonar-history.json")

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: organization already uses SonarQube languages, can inject rsonar results via Generic Issue Import: sonar-project.properties:
code: export_sonar_json(res, "sonar-issues.json") sonar.externalIssuesReportPaths=sonar-issues.json

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: GitLab CI JUnit artifacts:
code: export_sarif(res, "results.sarif") export_junit(res, "junit-results.xml")

title: Introduction to rsonar
text: Alternatives added value rsonar Complete CI/CD integration Understanding technical debt

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: Technical debt cost additional work caused shortcuts code: quick fixes, untested code, poor naming, unnecessary complexity. Like financial debt, accumulates interest repaid. rsonar quantifies debt estimated remediation minutes, using SQALE (Software Quality Assessment based Lifecycle Expectations) model used SonarQube.

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: total debt sum costs per category: Dtotal=Dlint_err+Dlint_warn+Dstyle+Dcoverage+DgpD_{total} = D_{lint\_err} + D_{lint\_warn} + D_{style} + D_{coverage} + D_{gp} default costs:

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: rating computed debt--base-effort ratio: ratio=DtotalNfiles×30 minratio = \frac{D_{total}}{N_{files} \times 30 \text{ min}} quality_score() exposes debt ratio easy--read percentage IDE usage: score=100×(1−min(1,ratio))score = 100 \times (1 - min(1, ratio))

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
code: library(rsonar)  res  <- sonar_analyse(".") debt <- debt_index(res) print(debt)  # Fast local score shown in IDE quality_score(res) ── rsonar Technical Debt ─────────────────────────────────── ℹ Estimated duration: 2.17h (130 min) ℹ SQALE rating: C  ── Breakdown by category ────────────────────────────────────              category issues minutes 1      Lint (errors)      2      60 2     Lint (warnings)     4      40 3     Best practices      1      20 4          Coverage       2      10

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: Default costs can adjusted match organization’s context:
code: # Stricter costs for a critical project debt <- debt_index(res,   cost_lint_error     = 60,   # 1h per error   cost_lint_warning   = 15,   cost_style          = 10,   cost_gp             = 30,   coverage_target     = 90,   # Target: 90%   cost_coverage_point = 8 )

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: Sort debt_minutes / number_of_corrections_needed ratio:
code: debt <- debt_index(res)  # Display categories with the most debt breakdown <- debt$breakdown[order(-debt$breakdown$minutes), ] print(breakdown)

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: existing project lot debt, adopt incremental approach:
code: # Week 1: fix lint errors (highest impact) # Week 2: add tests to increase coverage # Week 3: reformat the code (styler::style_dir) # Week 4: address warnings and goodpractice  # Track debt evolution over time debt_week_1  <- debt_index(res_before)$minutes debt_week_4  <- debt_index(res_after)$minutes reduction_pct <- round(100 * (1 - debt_week_4 / debt_week_1), 1) cat("Debt reduction:", reduction_pct, "%\n")

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: Use sonar_diff() see exactly changed two analysis runs: shows delta metrics, new issues introduced, issues fixed.
code: baseline <- sonar_analyse(".") # ... make improvements ... current  <- sonar_analyse(".") diff     <- sonar_diff(current, baseline) print(diff)

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: sonar_trend() appends analysis JSON history file, making easy monitor quality evolution across builds: .gitlab-ci.yml:
code: res <- sonar_analyse(".") sonar_trend(res, file = "rsonar-history.json") rsonar-report:   stage: report   when: always   script:     - |       Rscript -e "       library(rsonar)       res <- sonar_analyse('.')       sonar_trend(res)       sonar_report(res, output='rsonar-report.html', open=FALSE)       "   artifacts:     paths:       - rsonar-report.html       - rsonar-history.json     expire_in: 4 weeks

title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: .gitlab-ci.yml, expose rating metric:
code: rsonar-metrics:   script:     - |       Rscript -e "       library(rsonar)       res  <- sonar_analyse('.')       debt <- debt_index(res)       cat('SQALE rating:', debt\$rating, '\n')       cat('Debt minutes:', debt\$minutes, '\n')       # Fail if rating is D or E       if (debt\$rating %in% c('D', 'E'))         stop('Insufficient SQALE rating: ', debt\$rating)       "

what: Best practices for maintaining low debt
title: Understanding and Reducing Technical Debt
text: Configure .lintr start project use_rsonar_lintr() Format systematically commit: styler::style_dir("R") Write tests alongside code, Block regressions CI quality_gate(fail_on_error = TRUE) Don’t accumulate commented code — use Git history Name clearly functions variables — object_length_linter(30) best way manage technical debt accumulate .

title: Authors and Citation
text: Damien Dotta. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Damien Dotta (2026). rsonar: R Code Quality Analysis Inspired SonarQube. R package version 0.2.0, https://ddotta.github.io/rsonar/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rsonar: R Code Quality Analysis Inspired by SonarQube},   author = {{Damien Dotta}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://ddotta.github.io/rsonar/}, }

title: rsonar — R Code Quality
text: rsonar R equivalent SonarQube: centralizes code quality analysis single interactive report, estimates technical debt integrates natively GitLab CI GitHub Actions pipelines. quick local check (without CI/forge), run quality_score(".") display quality percentage directly IDE console. See repository illustrates features rsonar package. orchestrates four proven tools:

title: rsonar — R Code Quality
code: # From GitHub remotes::install_github("SSM-Agriculture/rsonar")

title: rsonar — R Code Quality
code: library(rsonar)  # Full analysis of an R package res <- sonar_analyse("path/to/my/package")  # Console summary print(res)  # Quick local quality percentage in IDE quality_score(res)  # Interactive HTML report sonar_report(res, output = "quality.html")  # Technical debt index (A to E) debt_index(res)  # Quality Gate (pass/fail like SonarQube) quality_gate(res, coverage_min = 80, lint_errors_max = 0)  # Compare two analyses (regression detection) diff <- sonar_diff(current_res, baseline_res)  # Track quality trends over time sonar_trend(res)  # Export SonarQube Generic Issue Import format export_sonar_json(res, "sonar-issues.json")  # Export SARIF for GitHub Code Scanning export_sarif(res, "results.sarif")  # Export JUnit XML (for GitLab CI artifacts) export_junit(res, "test-results.xml")

title: rsonar — R Code Quality
text: want instant feedback coding (without CI), run: Typical output:
code: library(rsonar)  # Fast local score (by default: no coverage/goodpractice for speed) quality_score(".") ── Quick Quality Score ───────────────────────────────────── ℹ Path   : /path/to/project ℹ Score  : 82.4% ℹ Rating : B ℹ Time   : 2026-04-21 10:15

title: rsonar — R Code Quality
text: See full documentation.

title: rsonar — R Code Quality
text: See CI/CD examples.

title: Estimate Technical Debt — debt_index
text: Computes technical debt index inspired SQALE model used SonarQube. index expressed estimated remediation minutes rating (excellent) E (critical).

title: Estimate Technical Debt — debt_index
code: debt_index(   x,   cost_lint_error = 30,   cost_lint_warning = 10,   cost_lint_style = 2,   cost_style = 5,   cost_gp = 20,   coverage_target = 80,   cost_coverage_point = 5 )

title: Estimate Technical Debt — debt_index
text: x rsonar_result object returned sonar_analyse(). cost_lint_error Cost minutes per lint issue type error. Default 30. cost_lint_warning Cost minutes per lint warning. Default 10. cost_lint_style Cost minutes per lint style violation. Default 2. cost_style Cost minutes per improperly formatted file (styler). Default 5. cost_gp Cost minutes per goodpractice failure. Default 20. coverage_target Target coverage %. Default 80. cost_coverage_point Cost minutes per missing coverage point target. Default 5.

title: Estimate Technical Debt — debt_index
text: rsonar_debt object (list) containing: minutes Total estimated debt minutes hours Total estimated debt hours rating SQALE rating: "", "B", "C", "D" "E" breakdown Data frame details category

title: Estimate Technical Debt — debt_index
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- sonar_analyse(".") d <- debt_index(res) print(d) # Total debt: 2.5h — Rating: B } # }

what: Define and Check a Quality Gate — quality_gate
title: Define and Check a Quality Gate — quality_gate
text: Reproduces behavior SonarQube Quality Gate: defines quality thresholds returns TRUE/FALSE based whether project meets . CI, R process exit code can set 1 block pipeline failure.

title: Define and Check a Quality Gate — quality_gate
code: quality_gate(   x,   coverage_min = 80,   lint_errors_max = 0,   lint_warnings_max = Inf,   style_issues_max = 0,   gp_fails_max = Inf,   rating_min = "C",   fail_on_error = FALSE )

title: Define and Check a Quality Gate — quality_gate
text: x rsonar_result object returned sonar_analyse(). coverage_min Minimum required coverage %. Default 80. NULL disable threshold. lint_errors_max Maximum number tolerated lint errors. Default 0. lint_warnings_max Maximum number tolerated lint warnings. Default Inf. style_issues_max Maximum number improperly formatted files. Default 0. gp_fails_max Maximum number goodpractice failures. Default Inf. rating_min Minimum required SQALE rating ("" "E"). Default "C". fail_on_error TRUE, stops R process quit(status = 1) gate fails. Useful CI. Default FALSE.

title: Define and Check a Quality Gate — quality_gate
text: rsonar_gate object (list) : passed TRUE thresholds met checks Detailed data frame check

title: Define and Check a Quality Gate — quality_gate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- sonar_analyse(".")  # Strict gate: 0 errors, 80% coverage gate <- quality_gate(res, coverage_min = 80, lint_errors_max = 0) print(gate)  # In CI: exit with code 1 on failure quality_gate(res, coverage_min = 80, fail_on_error = TRUE) } # }

what: Quick Quality Score for IDE Usage — quality_score
title: Quick Quality Score for IDE Usage — quality_score
text: Computes quick quality percentage score project, designed fast feedback directly IDE without CI platform.

title: Quick Quality Score for IDE Usage — quality_score
code: quality_score(   x = ".",   include_coverage = FALSE,   include_goodpractice = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Quick Quality Score for IDE Usage — quality_score
text: x path analyze (character) rsonar_result object. Default ".". include_coverage Logical. Include coverage quick analysis x path. Default FALSE speed. include_goodpractice Logical. Include goodpractice checks quick analysis x path. Default FALSE speed. verbose Logical. Show progress summary console. Default TRUE.

title: Quick Quality Score for IDE Usage — quality_score
text: object class rsonar_score fields: score (0-100), rating (-E), ratio, path, timestamp.

title: Quick Quality Score for IDE Usage — quality_score
text: can pass either: path R project/package, existing rsonar_result object. score derived technical debt ratio: score = 100 * (1 - min(1, debt_ratio)).

title: Quick Quality Score for IDE Usage — quality_score
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fast local check directly in IDE quality_score(".")  # Reuse an existing analysis res <- sonar_analyse(".") quality_score(res) } # }

what: rsonar: R Code Quality Analysis Inspired by SonarQube — rsonar-package
title: rsonar: R Code Quality Analysis Inspired by SonarQube — rsonar-package
text: Provides unified interface R code quality analysis inspired SonarQube. Combines lintr (static analysis), styler (code style), covr (test coverage) goodpractice (best practices) comprehensive quality reports. Estimates technical debt, detects code smells, integrates seamlessly CI/CD pipelines (GitLab CI, GitHub Actions). Produces HTML, JSON JUnit XML reports suitable SonarQube ingestion.

title: rsonar: R Code Quality Analysis Inspired by SonarQube — rsonar-package
text: Maintainer: Damien Dotta damien.dotta@live.fr

what: Complete R Project Quality Analysis — sonar_analyse
title: Complete R Project Quality Analysis — sonar_analyse
text: Main function rsonar. Orchestrates static analysis (lintr), style checking (styler), test coverage measurement (covr) packaging best practices (goodpractice), returns rsonar_result object summarizing results.

title: Complete R Project Quality Analysis — sonar_analyse
code: sonar_analyse(   path = ".",   include_lint = TRUE,   include_style = TRUE,   include_coverage = fs::dir_exists(fs::path(path, "tests")),   include_goodpractice = fs::file_exists(fs::path(path, "DESCRIPTION")),   exclude_pattern = "(\\.git|\\.ci|renv|packrat|vendor|node_modules|_snaps)",   lintr_config = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Complete R Project Quality Analysis — sonar_analyse
text: path Path R project package analyze. Defaults current directory. include_lint Logical. Enable lintr static analysis. Default TRUE. include_style Logical. Enable styler style checking. Default TRUE. include_coverage Logical. Enable covr coverage measurement. Default TRUE tests/ directory exists. include_goodpractice Logical. Enable goodpractice checks. Default TRUE DESCRIPTION file exists. exclude_pattern Regular expression exclude files. Default "(\\.git|\\.ci|renv|packrat|vendor|node_modules|_snaps)". lintr_config Path custom .lintr file. NULL, rsonar automatically looks .lintr path. verbose Logical. Show progress console. Default TRUE.

title: Complete R Project Quality Analysis — sonar_analyse
text: object class rsonar_result containing: lint List lintr issues (class lints) style Data frame files style issues coverage covr object NULL goodpractice goodpractice object NULL metrics Data frame consolidated metrics debt Technical debt estimate path Analyzed path timestamp Analysis date/time

title: Complete R Project Quality Analysis — sonar_analyse
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Analyze the current package res <- sonar_analyse(".") print(res)  # Analyze without coverage (faster) res <- sonar_analyse(".", include_coverage = FALSE)  # With a custom .lintr file res <- sonar_analyse(".", lintr_config = "custom.lintr") } # }

title: Compare Two rsonar Analyses — sonar_diff
text: Computes difference two rsonar_result objects, similar SonarQube's "New Code" analysis. useful detecting regressions improvements two analysis runs (e.g., pull request).

title: Compare Two rsonar Analyses — sonar_diff
code: sonar_diff(current, baseline)

title: Compare Two rsonar Analyses — sonar_diff
text: current rsonar_result object representing current state. baseline rsonar_result object representing baseline (e.g., main branch).

title: Compare Two rsonar Analyses — sonar_diff
text: rsonar_diff object (list) containing: delta Data frame metric deltas (metric, baseline, current, change) improved Logical. TRUE metrics improved stayed new_issues Lint issues present current baseline fixed_issues Lint issues present baseline current

title: Compare Two rsonar Analyses — sonar_diff
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ baseline <- sonar_analyse(".", include_coverage = FALSE) # ... make changes ... current  <- sonar_analyse(".", include_coverage = FALSE) diff     <- sonar_diff(current, baseline) print(diff) } # }

what: Generate an HTML Quality Report — sonar_report
title: Generate an HTML Quality Report — sonar_report
text: Produces interactive HTML report summarizing results sonar_analyse(): consolidated metrics, lint issues file navigation, style violations, test coverage technical debt.

title: Generate an HTML Quality Report — sonar_report
code: sonar_report(   x,   output = "rsonar_report.html",   title = "rsonar Quality Report",   open = interactive() )

title: Generate an HTML Quality Report — sonar_report
text: x rsonar_result object returned sonar_analyse(). output Path output HTML file. Default "rsonar_report.html". title Report title. Default "rsonar Quality Report". open Open report browser generation. Default interactive().

title: Generate an HTML Quality Report — sonar_report
text: path generated HTML file (invisibly).

title: Generate an HTML Quality Report — sonar_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- sonar_analyse(".") sonar_report(res, output = "quality.html") } # }

what: Record Analysis History for Trend Tracking — sonar_trend
title: Record Analysis History for Trend Tracking — sonar_trend
text: Appends current analysis results JSON history file, enabling debt trend tracking time. entry records timestamp, commit SHA (CI environment variables), key metrics SQALE rating. analogous SonarQube's project history.

title: Record Analysis History for Trend Tracking — sonar_trend
code: sonar_trend(x, file = "rsonar-history.json")

title: Record Analysis History for Trend Tracking — sonar_trend
text: x rsonar_result object returned sonar_analyse(). file Path JSON history file. Default "rsonar-history.json". Created automatically exist.

title: Record Analysis History for Trend Tracking — sonar_trend
text: new history entry (invisibly), list timestamp, commit, metrics debt fields.

title: Record Analysis History for Trend Tracking — sonar_trend
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- sonar_analyse(".") sonar_trend(res) sonar_trend(res, file = "quality-history.json") } # }

title: rsonar 0.2.0 (2026-04-20)
text: sonar_diff(): compare two analyses detect regressions improvements, similar SonarQube’s “New Code” analysis (#9) export_sarif(): export results SARIF format GitHub Code Scanning, VS Code Azure DevOps integration (#10) sonar_trend(): persist analysis history JSON file tracking quality metrics time (#11)

title: rsonar 0.2.0 (2026-04-20)
text: documentation rewritten English (roxygen, vignettes, README, pkgdown site) Vignettes updated sonar_diff(), export_sarif() sonar_trend() examples

title: rsonar 0.2.0 (2026-04-20)
text: Added quality_score() fast local IDE feedback quality percentage style checks longer require roxygen2 roxygen examples coverage checks reuse installed dependencies (clean = FALSE)

title: rsonar 0.1.0 (2026-04-20)
text: sonar_analyse(): complete R project analysis (lintr + styler + covr + goodpractice) (#1) sonar_report(): interactive HTML dashboard report (#2) quality_gate(): configurable quality thresholds CI exit code (#3) debt_index(): technical debt estimation using SQALE model (#4) export_sonar_json(): export SonarQube Generic Issue Import format (#5) export_junit(): JUnit XML export GitLab CI / GitHub Actions (#6) use_rsonar_lintr(): copy rsonar reference .lintr file (#7) use_rsonar_ci(): copy CI pipeline template (GitLab GitHub) (#8)

title: rsonar 0.1.0 (2026-04-20)
text: inst/templates/default.lintr: lintr configuration aligned SonarQube rules inst/templates/ci/gitlab-rsonar.yml: complete GitLab CI pipeline separate stages inst/templates/ci/github-rsonar.yml: complete GitHub Actions workflow

title: rsonar 0.1.0 (2026-04-20)
text: 4 vignettes: introduction, CI/CD, technical debt, alternatives pkgdown site Bootstrap 5
